evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Understanding of lineage, data contracts, canonical entities, and quality testing
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Repeated emphasis on governed data products, certified metrics, data contracts, and quality testing with no velocity/speed language makes this rigour-dominated.
evidence.domain_risk: designing governed data products, writing SQL for transformation and validation
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The role affects internal analytics and AI capability decisions without explicit regulatory or financial-reporting language, keeping domain risk at moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: building data products, semantic layers, or certified metrics
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named practices already in place (semantic layer, certified metrics, data contracts) alongside a mandate to extend into AI analytics indicate a growing, mid-stage data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: designing governed data products, writing SQL for transformation and validation, building semantic definitions and certified metrics
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Precise responsibilities (governed data products, semantic definitions, certified metrics, embedded BI) with explicit disclaimer distinguishing it from traditional BI show clear hiring-manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role builds shared data infrastructure (semantic layer, certified metrics) consumed across domain teams, an internal_data orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The candidate is expected to design and build foundational data products and semantic definitions, a strategic ownership verb.
evidence.ai_role: supporting embedded BI and future AI analytics
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate builds governed data foundations that downstream AI analytics capabilities will consume, making them an ai_enabler.
evidence.testing_framing: Understanding of lineage, data contracts, canonical entities, and quality testing
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Quality testing and data contracts appear as required understanding tied directly to the governed-data-product responsibility, read as an owned practice rather than a bare tool mention.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Governance and certified metrics reflect a quality/trust concern but without explicit compliance or regulatory framing, placing it at moderate.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: This is not a traditional BI or dashboarding role
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The explicit reframing away from traditional BI toward building new governed data foundations signals greenfield work.
evidence.loss_aversion: designing governed data products, writing SQL for transformation and validation — governance framing focuses on trust in metrics, not regulatory risk.
